title: Forbidden or high-risk PHP functions
summary: Some functions are disallowed or high risk in WordPress.org plugin review because they indicate dangerous execution behavior or insecure file handling.

detection.notes: Current repo coverage is a small subset of the official rule surface.

guidance.why_it_matters: These functions often indicate behavior that is insecure, bypasses WordPress APIs, or violates repository expectations.
guidance.fix: Replace dangerous execution and upload handling with WordPress APIs such as wp_handle_upload() and remove unsafe constants or dynamic code execution patterns.
guidance.false_positive_risk: Low to medium. Comments or examples may still be flagged by simple text search.
examples: Avoid eval() and other dynamic execution functions entirely.
